title: Variety in Exercise May Extend Lifespan, Study Finds
summary: A new study published in BMJ Medicine suggests that engaging in a variety of physical activities could be more beneficial for longevity than just focusing on total exercise volume. Researchers analyzed data from over 111,000 participants from two long-term studies spanning more than 30 years. They found that people who participated in different types of exercise, such as walking, jogging, swimming, and weight training, had a lower risk of death from all causes, including cardiovascular disease and cancer. The benefits leveled off after a certain amount of activity, indicating an optimal range. Variety itself was linked to reduced mortality regardless of total exercise time. Walking was the most common activity, and men were more likely to jog or run. Overall, those with higher activity levels were healthier, with lower rates of smoking, high blood pressure, and obesity. The researchers emphasize that while total activity matters, incorporating diverse activities may provide additional health benefits.

keywords.explanation: long life or duration of life
diseases of the heart and blood vessels
death rate or risk of dying

questions.question: What is the main finding of the study?
options: B. Variety in exercise reduces death risk
C. Total exercise volume is the only factor
D. Exercise has no effect on lifespan

questions.question: What was the most common exercise reported?
Which group had lower rates of smoking and high blood pressure?
options: D. Those who did not exercise

questions.question: What does 'variety' refer to in the study?
options: A. Doing the same exercise daily
B. Doing different types of activities
D. Exercising alone or in groups

questions.question: How long did the study follow participants?
What is a MET score used for?
options: B. Measuring energy used during activity

background_read: Physical activity is known to reduce the risk of chronic diseases and early death. This study adds that variety in exercise may offer extra benefits beyond total amount.
Article_Structure: Main Points: The study shows that variety in physical activities is associated with lower mortality risk, independent of total exercise volume. Purpose: To investigate whether engaging in different types of exercise provides additional longevity benefits beyond total activity. Evidence Evaluation: The study uses large, long-term datasets from the Nurses' Health Study and Health Professionals Follow-Up Study, providing robust evidence. Author Credibility: The research was published in a peer-reviewed medical journal, BMJ Medicine. Methodology: Researchers analyzed self-reported physical activity data over 30 years, using MET scores to quantify activity levels and variety.

perspectives.description: Encouraging people to try different exercises could improve population health.
